title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Collection Query
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 9791 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Sitemap
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-qnlsy2" that uses case 6968 to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Sitemap.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Sitemap into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Sitemap, keep Feed Sitemap connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-qnlsy2
# Competency: ssg-feed-sitemap.
# Case 6968: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_feed_sitemap_qnlsy2(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_qnlsy2 = "qnlsy2-2yslnq-6968"
    assert evidence_variant_qnlsy2
    evidence_axes_qnlsy2 = (
        "readerqnlsy2",
        "source6968",
        "parse2yslnq",
        "routeqnlsy26968",
        "artifact6968qnlsy2",
        "recovery2yslnq6968",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_qnlsy2)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
